claim_focus: LIMEN is an evidence-infrastructure method rather than an incident ranking or prevalence study.
evidence_used_now: Frozen paper snapshot and reviewer-objection appendix already encode the method-not-ranking ceiling.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: Reviewers may still read the abstract as anecdotal corpus framing if the evidence-infrastructure sentence is detached from claim-ceiling language.
paper_thesis_use: Abstract/contribution spine for the first LIMEN paper and thesis methods chapter.
dashboard_hook: Landing-card text for dashboard/about panel explaining why the observatory encodes limits.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep the abstract tied to PCR-001 whenever title/subtitle or opening sentences are revised.

claim_focus: The frozen manuscript denominator is 21 case threads with 15 stronger bounded examples, 6 limitations/discovery rows, and 6 translation-dependent rows.
evidence_used_now: The denominator and translation counts are already frozen in the synchronized snapshot and safe aggregate bundle.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: Any silent import of post-freeze sidecars or live 25-lineage drift would weaken reviewer trust in count discipline.
paper_thesis_use: Methods/data section and thesis provenance note on freeze discipline.
dashboard_hook: Denominator badge plus translation overlay for frozen-paper dashboard mode.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Preserve the 21-lineage freeze note and keep DoNotPay/Workado/Springer/Datatilsynet outside denominator prose unless full recomputation is chosen.

claim_focus: Public-source-only access, duplicate control, evidence classes, and claim boundaries are substantive method rules rather than housekeeping.
evidence_used_now: Existing duplicate, source-depth, and negative-result artifacts already support the method as a bounded evidence protocol.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: If duplicate control or blocked-source handling is summarized without the sidecar controls, reviewers can recast the method as ad hoc curation.
paper_thesis_use: Core methods section and thesis evidence-infrastructure chapter.
dashboard_hook: Method tabs: duplicate-control QA, source-depth ladder, and blocked-source registry.
next_smallest_publishability_move: When revising methods prose, cite one duplicate-control and one negative-results support artifact explicitly in-text or appendix notes.

claim_focus: The corpus contains non-interchangeable source families, so composition should be read as observability boundary rather than completeness.
evidence_used_now: Figure 1 plus its source-family claim-ceiling bundle already support bounded source-composition prose.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: If Figure 1 is reused without thin-family blocker language, seeded families can look equally mature or complete.
paper_thesis_use: Main results figure and thesis section on source-strategy breadth.
dashboard_hook: Source-family saturation map with blocker legend.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep Figure 1/Table 1 control-bundle references attached whenever Results 4.1 wording is tightened.

claim_focus: The set contains both stronger public records and thinner single-source leads, and the paper labels them differently.
evidence_used_now: The refreshed 21-lineage safe funnel supports the stronger-versus-thinner evidence maturity claim.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: Dropping the denominator/confidence banner would let readers overread the funnel as prevalence or uniform certainty.
paper_thesis_use: Headline evidence-maturity result for paper, dashboard, and thesis chapter.
dashboard_hook: Evidence-tier funnel with denominator and confidence overlays.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep the refreshed safe funnel, not the older staged funnel, as the sole count-bearing support for Results 4.2.

claim_focus: Counts depend on duplicate review, label-collision control, and non-merge discipline before totals are reported.
evidence_used_now: The live Figure 4 contract now fully materializes all graph-eligible reviewed edges while keeping graph-versus-ledger scope explicit.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: If reviewed-not-duplicate overlays are read as recurrence evidence, the figure becomes a substantive incident graph instead of a QA graph.
paper_thesis_use: Methods-quality results block and thesis appendix on join safety.
dashboard_hook: Duplicate-control graph legend and join-safety QA badge.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Preserve the non-recurrence ceiling and graph-versus-ledger wording whenever Figure 4 prose or legends are reused.

claim_focus: Visible, thin, and zero-seed categories are results about corpus shape, not real-world absence.
evidence_used_now: The taxonomy heatmap is publication-usable only with the live 11/1/3 support-state wording and fragility/zero-seed sidecars.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: Without the sidecars, reviewers may read zero-seed rows as real-world absence or flatten candidate-only and authority-backed rows together.
paper_thesis_use: Core novelty/gap result and thesis taxonomy chapter.
dashboard_hook: Taxonomy heatmap plus zero-seed and fragility overlays.
next_smallest_publishability_move: When touching Results 4.4, carry the live support-state split and explicit zero-seed warning into caption or adjacent prose.

claim_focus: Official-looking or authoritative sources still support different ceilings; blocked, summary-only, accusation-stage, and sidecar records remain methodologically valuable negatives.
evidence_used_now: The manuscript narrative is supported, but reviewer resilience improves if Table 1A/Table 1E or appendix citations travel with the Figure 5 and negative-results discussion.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: Without explicit table/appendix anchors, reviewers may read 'official source' as a uniform document-depth class or treat blocked/403 surfaces as missing diligence.
paper_thesis_use: Main methodological result, negative-results section, and thesis claim-support map for source-depth ceilings.
dashboard_hook: Source-depth ladder; negative-results card; blocked-source tooltip.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Add or preserve explicit appendix/table citations near Table 2 / Figure 5 prose when polishing the manuscript again.

claim_focus: The paper is small, not prevalence-ready, and still lacks a true second-coder reliability result.
evidence_used_now: Current artifacts support limitation prose and clearly separate protocol dry-run from completed inter-rater reliability.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: The main risk is accidental overstatement of the current audit as reliability evidence.
paper_thesis_use: Limitations section and reviewer-response appendix; thesis reproducibility note.
dashboard_hook: Audit-status badge and second-coder pipeline card.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Do not strengthen reliability wording until an actual independent second-coder pass is completed.

claim_focus: Selected public exemplars are safe only as bounded anchors and sidecars, not as denominator-changing additions or broad legal/compliance exemplars.
evidence_used_now: Current cited exemplars are defendable when their sidecar or bounded-source roles remain explicit.
reviewer_risk_if_reused_without_control: A reader could otherwise infer that all cited examples share the same denominator status or support harm/compliance conclusions.
paper_thesis_use: Data/public-source availability note and thesis provenance appendix.
dashboard_hook: Example-card panel with sidecar-role and source-depth metadata.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep public-source exemplar notes posture-specific and preserve sidecar/non-denominator language for FTC and Datatilsynet examples.
